channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya as thousands turn out to mourn former PM Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu ahead of the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel received coffin of missing hostage handed over to Red Cross, prime minister's office says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel received the remains of another hostage from Gaza on Friday, the prime minister’s office said, a handover from Hamas as the militant group worked to shore up a ceasefire by using bulldozers in a search for bodies under the rubble in the war-scarred enclave.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Dozens of reporters stationed at the Pentagon walked out this week and surrendered their access badges rather than sign on to new rules imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth they said would restrict how they do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy pitches Trump on Ukraine drone-for-Tomahawk missile exchange as president weighs escalation concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump shows interest in a potential drone deal with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy but calls Tomahawk missiles a significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polish court blocks extradition and frees Ukrainian suspected in Nord Stream pipeline blasts
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A Polish court on Friday blocked the extradition to Germany of a Ukrainian man suspected of involvement in the 2022 attack on the Nord Stream gas pipelines and ordered his release, a ruling that was welcomed by Poland's prime minister.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA CIOBANU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Watch live: Trump meets with Ukraine's Zelensky at White House
item.description: President Trump will host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House on Friday for the third time since returning to office in January. The first visit was a train wreck; the second, which involved European leaders, went more smoothly.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Political crisis in France eases for now as prime minister survives no-confidence vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s latest political crisis eased — for now — when Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu survived two consecutive no-confidence votes on Thursday, averting another government collapse and giving President Emmanuel Macron a respite before an even tougher fight over the national budget.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia targets Ukraine energy facilities ahead of Trump meeting with Zelensky
item.description: Russia hit Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles Thursday, a day ahead of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s meeting with President Trump at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Remains of last female Hamas hostage and IDF soldier handed over to Israel
item.description: Israel received two more deceased hostages' bodies, including that of Inbar Hayman, who was considered to be the last female hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: State Department aware of reports after American tourists attacked, 1 killed in popular seaside destination
item.description: State Department aware of reports from coastal town near Lisbon, Portugal, as Cascais police investigate after two Americans attacked, leaving one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan and Taliban agree to 48-hour ceasefire after renewed fighting kills dozens
item.description: A temporary ceasefire has been agreed to after border violence escalated between Pakistan and Afghanistan over the weekend as both sides blame each other for attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Parents of 2 US citizens demand return of their sons’ bodies and all deceased hostages held by Hamas in Gaza
item.description: Two American citizens, Itay Chen and Omer Neutra, remain among deceased hostages held by Hamas as terror group violates hostage exchange agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Kenyan premier Raila Odinga, a key figure in African democracy efforts, dies at 80
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Raila Odinga, a former prime minister of Kenya and perennial presidential candidate whose populist campaigns challenged one-party rule, rattled authorities and gave him outsized influence on political life in the East African country, died Wednesday of a heart attack while trav
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Russia using new, more precise drone tech to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in early 2022, railway officials have publicly reported about roughly one attack on railways per week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese airlines protest US plan to ban their flights over Russian airspace
item.description: The U.S. side has said such flights give Chinese airlines an unfair cost advantage over American carriers, which cannot cross through Russian airspace.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A new form of tourism: US residents seek tattoos in Mexico
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – U.S. residents often cite lower prices and personalized attention when asked why they travel to see doctors and dentists in Mexico. The same holds true for tattoo artists.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American who was on death row in China details horrifying experiences
item.description: Speaking in his first television interview since being released in November 2024 as part of a prisoner swap, Swidan detailed his experience on "Elizabeth Vargas Reports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russian aerial attack hits a Ukrainian hospital, days before Zelenskyy meets Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched powerful glide bombs and drones against Ukraine’s second-largest city in overnight attacks, hitting a hospital and wounding seven people, an official said Tuesday, as European military aid for Kyiv dropped sharply and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensk
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Freed hostages' loved ones thank Trump for reuniting them after more than two years
item.description: Several families of the freed Israeli hostages thanked President Donald Trump and his team for brokering the Israel-Hamas deal that brought them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: African island nation plunged into political chaos as president flees country, military seizes control
item.description: Madagascar's president fled the country, and the military is taking over, according to an Associated Press report about the African nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: How the US brokered the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas saw the release of Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners and detainees.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WGN 9
